metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_2552
metadata.comment:
Rationale: From Results section:

"Consistent with the results from yeast two-hybrid interaction assay, we found that
NR2C, but not NR2A and NR2B, coimmunoprecipitated with 14-3-33ε (Figure 1D), demonstrating that NR2C is a specific binding partner for 14-3-33ε in vivo."

"Surface expression of NR2C WT was dramatically decreased upon cotransfection with 14-3-33ε dn (Figure 4), indicating that the interaction between NR2C and 14-3-3ε is required for NR2C trafficking. In addition, surface expression of NR2C S1096A was much less than that of NR2C WT when 14-3-33ε was coexpressed
and was further reduced upon coexpression with 14-3-33ε dn (Figure 4), suggesting that S1096 is not the sole molecular determinant in 14-3-33-regulated NR2C trafficking.". Experimental description: From fig 1d:
Adult rat cerebella were solubilized, and 14-3-33ε was immunoprecipitated from lysates with anti-14-3-33 antibodies or IgG antibodies as a control.

From fig 4:
Cerebellar granule cells were cotransfected with NR2C containing an extracellular GFP protein tag (GFP-NR2C) or GFP-NR2C S1096A and Myc-14-3-33 WT or Myc-14-3-33ε dn.
